results.title: Acute generalized exanthematous pustulosis caused by hydroxychloroquine in a patient with rheumatoid arthritis and CARD14 mutation: Case report.
results.abstract: Acute generalized exanthematous pustulosis (AGEP) is a serious adverse skin reaction characterized by the rapid appearance of densely distributed, small, sterile pustules with erythema. However, its pathogenesis is not fully understood. Hydroxychloroquine is widely used for the treatment of autoimmune diseases. Some patients presenting with AGEP have IL36RN and CARD14 gene mutations. Our report describes a patient with rheumatoid arthritis and AGEP associated with hydroxychloroquine and a newly discovered CARD14 gene mutation.
